metadata.title: Differences in Circulating Gluconeogenic Substrates During Short-Term Fasting in Men, Women, and Children
metadata.study_design: Comparative fasting study with three groups: 10 adult men fasted for 86 hr, 10 adult women fasted for 86 hr, and 15 prepubertal children fasted for 30 hr
metadata.demographics: Men (n=10, age 24.0±1.9 yr, height 181±2 cm, weight 73.7±2.7 kg); Women (n=10, age 23.7±1.1 yr, height 168.0±1.3 cm, weight 58.3±1.7 kg); Children (n=15, boys/girls=6/9, age 6.1±0.8 yr, height 114.2±5.9 cm, weight 21.5±2.5 kg)

evidence.quote: To determine whether the differences in fasting glucose responses of men, women and children could be related to differences in glucoregulatory hormone secretion or availability of circulating gluconeogenic substrates, 20 adults (10 men and 10 women) were fasted for 86 hr and 15 children (6.1 _+ 0.8 yr, mean + SE) were fasted for 30 hr.

Table 1. Demographic Data of the Subjects Studied Age (yr) Height (cm) Weight (kg) Men (n = 10) 24.0 _+ 1.9 181 ± 2 73.7 ± 2.7 Women (n = 10) 23.7 ± 1.1 168.0 _+ 1.3 58.3 ± 1.7 Children (boys/girls = 6/9) 6.1 _+ 0.8 114.2 ± 5.9 21.5 ± 2.5
20 adults (10 men and 10 women) were fasted for 86 hr
all had fasting glucose concentrations < 110 mg/dl

methods_participants.label: Inclusion Criteria - Carbohydrate Intake
methods_participants.value: Weight-maintaining diet; adults consuming at least 200 gm carbohydrate per day for three days prior to study

evidence.quote: The adults were consuming at least 200 gm carbohydrate per day for three days prior to the study

methods_participants.label: Fasting Protocol - Timing
methods_participants.value: Fasting following evening meal with blood sampling at 1800-2000 hr, 0800 hr, and subsequently at 4 hr intervals

evidence.quote: Bloods were obtained between 1800-2000 hr, 0800 hr, and subsequently at 4 hr intervals
Plasma glucagon, insulin, and growth hormone concentrations were determined by radioimmunoassays

methods_participants.label: Amino Acid Assay Method
methods_participants.method: Spinco-Beckman 119 Amino Acid Analyzer using standard physiologic methodology

evidence.quote: Amino acid determinations were performed on Spinco-Beckman 119 Amino Acid Analyzer using standard physiologic methodology
cortisol by a protein binding assay

methods_participants.label: Blood Collection Volume
methods_participants.value: 0.5 ml precipitated immediately with equal volume of cold 3M perchloric acid

evidence.quote: (1) 2 ml of blood were placed in 750 IU of Trasylol (FMA Pharmaceuticals)
on the 0, 14, 22, and 30 hr fasted samples of five children

methods_participants.label: Exclusion Criteria - Diabetes Family History
methods_participants.value: None of the subjects had a family history of diabetes

evidence.quote: None of the subjects had a family history of diabetes

methods_participants.label: Children Selection Criteria
methods_participants.value: Selected from patients who had symptoms compatible with hypoglycemia and were fasted as part of clinical evaluation; none previously had documented hypoglycemia, nor developed symptoms or signs of hypoglycemia during evaluation

evidence.quote: The 15 children were selected from patients who had had symptoms which were compatible with hypoglycemia and were fasted as part of their clinical evaluation. None previously had documented hypoglycemia, nor developed symptoms or signs of hypoglycemia during their evaluation.

narrative_results.statement: Glucose concentrations were similar in the three groups studied during the first 18 hr of fasting

evidence.quote: Glucose concentrations were similar in the three groups studied during the first 18 hr of fasting (Fig. 1).

narrative_results.statement: An inverse relationship was observed between plasma glucose and beta-hydroxybutyrate concentrations in each group

evidence.quote: An inverse relationship (p < 0.001) was observed between plasma glucose and beta-hydroxybutyrate concentrations in each group (r = 0.934, 0.925, 0.961, men, women and children, respectively).

Pass 1 specifies 2 ml blood sample while Pass 2 specifies 4-5 mL; these are incompatible collection volumes
Pass 1 describes blood sample collection with perchloric acid precipitation; Pass 2 describes amino acid sampling in children with fasting times. These are different aspects of the methodology.

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Beta-hydroxybutyrate and acetoacetate concentrations rose more rapidly and to higher values in the children (3.75 _+ 0.35 mM and 1.26 _+ 0.12 mM, respectively) than in the adults
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Beta-hydroxybutyrate and acetoacetate concentrations rose more rapidly and to higher values in the children (3.75 _+ 0.35 mM and 1.26 _+ 0.12 mM, respectively) than in the adults (0.91 _ 0.15 mM and 0.38 +_ 0.06 mM, respectively in the men and 1.68 _+ 0.20 mM and 0.62 _+ 0.06 mM in the women) over the initial 30 hr fasting (p < 0.001).

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Blood lactate concentrations were higher in children than in adults between 6 and 30 hr of fasting
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Between 6 and 30 hr of fasting, blood lactate concentrations were higher in children than in adults

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: The rate of fall and absolute decrease in alanine concentrations during the first 30 hr of fasting are greater in children than in adults
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Plasma alanine concentrations fell more rapidly and to a greater absolute decrease in children than in adults over the first 30 hr of fasting

semantic_groups.rationale: Both describe 15 children aged 6.1 ± 0.8 years; only minor label variation (Participant vs Study Population)
Both state 86 hours fasting duration for adults; only unit abbreviation differs (hours vs hr)
Both state 30 hours fasting duration for children; only unit abbreviation differs (hours vs hr)
Both specify fasting glucose < 110 mg/dl; only singular/plural variation in criteria label
Both require at least 200 gm carbohydrate per day for three days prior; pass_2 adds context about weight-maintaining diet and specifies adults
Pass_1 labels as inclusion criterion while pass_2 labels as exclusion criterion; logically opposite categorizations of the same criterion
Pass_1 labels as inclusion criterion while pass_2 labels as exclusion criterion; logically opposite categorizations
Both describe blood sampling at 1800-2000 hr, 0800 hr, then at 4-hour intervals; pass_2 adds context about evening meal timing (1700-1800 hr)
Pass 2 is empty; only Pass 1 contains information about fasting initiation time
Both describe the same glucose assay method using microfluorometric determination; minor wording variations only
Both identify Spinco-Beckman 119 Amino Acid Analyzer; Pass 1 includes methodology detail while Pass 2 uses generic 'amino acid analyzer' term
Both specify radioimmunoassay for glucagon; singular/plural variation is negligible
Both specify radioimmunoassay for insulin; singular/plural variation is negligible
Both specify radioimmunoassay for growth hormone; singular/plural variation is negligible
Both specify protein binding assay for cortisol; identical information
Pass 1 specifies 2 ml blood sample while Pass 2 specifies 4-5 mL; these are incompatible collection volumes
Pass 1 describes blood sample collection with perchloric acid precipitation; Pass 2 describes amino acid sampling in children with fasting times. These are different aspects of the methodology.
Pass 1 contains information about blood collection anticoagulant; Pass 2 is empty.
Both describe amino acid sampling in 6 men and 6 women at all sampling times. Minor wording differences ('determined at all sampling times in' vs 'all sampling times') do not affect meaning.
Pass 1 contains information about amino acid sampling in children with specific fasting times; Pass 2 is empty.
Both describe the same participant population: 10 men and 10 women. Only difference is labeling ('Participant Population - Adults' vs 'Study Population - Adults').
Identical text across both extractions
Identical text across both extractions
Identical text across both extractions
Same fact about inverse relationship; only metadata differs (comparison vs inverse)
Same fact with different word order; pass_2 fronts the time period
Same fact about alanine concentrations; pass_1 uses 'rate of fall and absolute decrease' while pass_2 uses 'fell more rapidly and to a greater absolute decrease'
Identical text across both extractions
Identical text across both extractions
